title: Heron Therapeutics, Inc. Q4 2025 Earnings Call Summary
content:
Heron Therapeutics, Inc. Q4 2025 Earnings Call Summary - Moby

Strategic Execution and Performance Drivers

The company successfully completed a financing round that eliminated a long-standing capital structure overhang, allowing management to focus entirely on commercial execution. Acute Care performance reached a strategic inflection point in Q4 2025, with ZYNRELEF and APONVIE delivering 48% and 97% year-over-year revenue growth, respectively. Operational improvements such as the CrossLink IGNITE distributor incentive program and the introduction of the vial access needle (VAN) significantly reduced hospital workflow friction. The implementation of permanent J-Codes for ZYNRELEF and APONVIE has improved reimbursement clarity, facilitating broader adoption across hospitals and ambulatory surgery centers. Oncology performance remained a stable revenue base, with CINVANTI showing resilience against competitive pressures while the company continues the planned wind-down of SUSTOL. Management is pivoting to a high-growth strategy by expanding sales capacity in priority geographies that exhibit favorable market access and established distributor relationships.

2026 Outlook and Strategic Initiatives

Full year 2026 guidance projects net product sales between $173 million and $183 million, with adjusted EBITDA expected to remain positive between $10 million and $20 million. The company plans to increase commercial investments starting mid-2026, which may temporarily moderate EBITDA growth to prioritize long-term revenue acceleration and free cash flow in 2027. ZYNRELEF's prefilled syringe (PFS) presentation is undergoing 12 months of stability testing, with a targeted FDA approval and launch window in mid- to late 2027. APONVIE adoption is expected to accelerate in the second half of 2026 as its inclusion in the Fifth Consensus Guidelines for PONV management begins to influence hospital P&T committee cycles. The NOPAIN Act is anticipated to serve as a significant tailwind as provider awareness of separate reimbursement for non-opioid treatments increases throughout 2026.

Risk Factors and Structural Changes

The Oncology franchise is undergoing a structural shift as SUSTOL is phased out through 2026, leading to a projected modest decline in total oncology revenue. CINVANTI faces ongoing competitive pricing dynamics in the clinic setting, though management expects to offset this through volume growth in the hospital segment. The transition to the prefilled syringe (PFS) will require careful inventory management of existing vial presentations to ensure a seamless market switch in 2027. Ongoing patent litigation regarding CINVANTI remains a factor, though management expressed confidence following the conclusion of trial briefing.

Story Continues

Q&A Session Summary

Our analysts just identified a stock with the potential to be the next Nvidia. Tell us how you invest and we'll show you why it's our #1 pick. Tap here.

Impact of NOPAIN Act on ZYNRELEF growth trajectory

Management noted that 2025 was primarily an education year for providers regarding the NOPAIN Act, and they expect it to be a strong tailwind moving forward. The act helps remove financial friction by allowing institutions to receive separate reimbursement rather than absorbing costs within a surgical bundle.

Strategic value of APONVIE's inclusion in clinical guidelines

Inclusion in the Fifth Consensus Guidelines provides clinical validity that is essential for Pharmacy and Therapeutics (P&T) committee approvals. Management expects a 6-to-9-month lag before guideline inclusion fully translates into updated hospital order sets and sustained long-term adoption.

Status and expectations for CINVANTI patent litigation

The company has finished briefing in the Slayback litigation and expects a decision from the U.S. District Court of Delaware before the 30-month stay expires. Management remains confident in the case presented at trial regarding their formulation and Orange Book patents.

title: Heron Therapeutics outlines $173M–$183M 2026 sales targets amid acute care momentum and expanded commercial investment
content:
Earnings Call Insights: Heron Therapeutics (HRTX) Q4 2025

MANAGEMENT VIEW

 	* CEO Craig Collard emphasized the "successful completion of our financing," describing it as a "meaningful derisking event" that enables the company to focus on "commercial execution, product expansion and delivering sustained growth." Collard highlighted strong operational and financial performance, with full year net revenues of approximately $155 million and adjusted EBITDA of $14.7 million, noting, "We delivered adjusted EBITDA of $14.7 million, exceeding our previously communicated guidance range of $9 million to $13 million." 
 	* Collard described multiple strategic initiatives in the Acute Care portfolio, including the launch of the CrossLink IGNITE program, the introduction of the vial access needle (VAN), and implementation of a new J-Code for ZYNRELEF, supporting broader hospital adoption. For APONVIE, a new dedicated sales team (IBM group) was established, and the product was included in the Fifth Consensus Guidelines for PONV management.
 	* Collard reported, "In Q4, ZYNRELEF delivered 48% net revenue growth compared to Q4 of 2024, while APONVIE grew 97% over the same period. Altogether, our Acute Care franchise increased more than 57% year-over-year in the quarter."
 	* Collard stated that the company will "accelerate the expansion of our commercial team in key markets across the country," with targeted investments potentially moderating EBITDA growth but aimed at maximizing near-term commercial productivity and pulling forward revenue.
 	* Executive VP & COO Mark Hensley reported, "Acute Care net sales were $16.3 million in the fourth quarter, up from $12.3 million in the third quarter, an increase of about 33%."
 	* Hensley explained that the CrossLink IGNITE program, VAN rollout, and permanent J-Codes were driving greater hospital adoption and streamlined reimbursement. He added, "Ordering accounts continue to expand as well. Operationally, we launched the dedicated APONVIE sales team on July 1, focused on high potential hospital accounts."
 	* Executive VP & CFO Ira Duarte stated, "Total net product sales for 2025 reached $154.9 million, an increase over 2024, driven primarily by the exceptional performance of our lead product, ZYNRELEF, which delivered 48% year-over-year revenue growth in the fourth quarter alone."
 	* Duarte added, "We continue to shift our product mix towards our higher-growth assets while maintaining strong and consistent gross margins."

OUTLOOK

 	* Duarte provided 2026 guidance, stating, "Our 2026 guidance reflects this confidence, net product sales of $173 million to $183 million and adjusted EBITDA of $10 million to $20 million, demonstrating continued profitability through a year of commercial expansions."
 	* Management indicated that increased commercial investments in 2026 are "warranted given the compelling long-term return profile," and these are expected to "drive meaningful growth in top line revenue."
 	* The company anticipates EBITDA positivity in 2026 while focusing on targeted growth investments and expects free cash flow generation in 2027.

FINANCIAL RESULTS

 	* Full year adjusted EBITDA reached $14.7 million, above prior guidance.
 	* Gross margin for the year was approximately 73%.
 	* ZYNRELEF net sales in Q4 were $12.5 million, up from $9.3 million in Q3.
 	* APONVIE net sales were $3.8 million in Q4, compared to $3 million in Q3.
 	* Oncology franchise generated just over $105 million in net revenue for the year, with a 7.8% decline attributed mainly to the planned SUSTOL wind down.

Q&A

 	* Serge Belanger, Needham: Asked about CINV franchise expectations and the NOPAIN Act. Collard responded, "We continue to think that we're going to -- we'll grow in unit volume... But at the same time, we're going to get some price erosion. So -- but sales should stay relatively flat throughout the year." On the NOPAIN Act, Hensley replied, "We believe the NOPAIN Act will continue to be certainly very beneficial to us... That, combined with the permanent J-Code for ZYNRELEF, certainly will remove friction for our institutions."
 	* Brandon Folkes, H.C. Wainwright: Asked about the VAN rollout and its impact. Hensley noted, "Our growth strategy for this year is 2 parts really... that's going deeper into hospitals... Certainly, the VAN has been a big part of removing... challenges on the preparation side. That's largely been eliminated."
 	* Carl Byrnes, Northland: Asked about the Slayback litigation and NOPAIN Act awareness. Melissa Jarel stated, "We're really confident with the case that we made at trial... we expect a decision before the 30-month stay." On NOPAIN Act awareness, Collard admitted, "... we were a little bit surprised" by the slow uptake and are "expanding some of our team" to support education.
 	* Byrnes also inquired about the impact of Fifth Consensus Guidelines on APONVIE. Hensley estimated a "timeline to that is probably back half of the year," with guideline-driven adoption expected to ramp up after a typical 6-month P&T approval cycle.

SENTIMENT ANALYSIS

 	* Analysts were generally positive but pressed for more detail on the pace and sustainability of acute care growth, the implementation of the NOPAIN Act, and competitive risks for oncology.
 	* Management maintained a confident and optimistic tone, with Collard stating, "...we are well positioned to continue this trajectory as we move into 2026."
 	* Compared to the previous quarter, management's confidence was more pronounced, with greater emphasis on recent momentum and strategic investments.
 	* Analysts' tone remained constructive but focused on execution and risk, mirroring the previous quarter's cautious optimism.

QUARTER-OVER-QUARTER COMPARISON

 	* 2026 guidance was raised to $173 million–$183 million in net product sales, compared to the previous guidance of $153 million–$163 million for 2025.
 	* Commercial expansion and investment became a more central theme, with increased hiring and targeted deployment highlighted for 2026 versus mainly structural changes in Q3.
 	* Acute Care sales acceleration outpaced the prior quarter, with ZYNRELEF and APONVIE both delivering higher sequential and year-over-year growth.
 	* Analysts' questions shifted from implementation details to the sustainability of growth and the impact of new reimbursement pathways.
 	* Management's tone signaled heightened confidence, particularly around the durability of recent commercial gains and the effectiveness of targeted investments.

RISKS AND CONCERNS

 	* Management acknowledged ongoing pricing pressure and competition in the oncology franchise, with CINVANTI and SUSTOL both subject to potential price erosion and account losses.
 	* The company cited the need for ongoing education and awareness-building around the NOPAIN Act, admitting that uptake had lagged expectations but is now accelerating with expanded support.
 	* Pending litigation regarding CINVANTI was addressed, with management expressing confidence in the case and expecting a decision before the 30-month stay.
 	* Increased commercial investments may temporarily moderate EBITDA growth.

FINAL TAKEAWAY

Heron Therapeutics delivered strong fourth quarter and full-year 2025 results, highlighted by double-digit growth in its Acute Care franchise and the successful elimination of its financing overhang. Management raised 2026 guidance and is accelerating commercial expansion in key markets, targeting $173 million to $183 million in net product sales while maintaining EBITDA profitability. Strategic initiatives such as new J-Codes, product launches, and inclusion in clinical guidelines are supporting broad adoption, though ongoing competitive and pricing pressures in oncology and the need for continued provider education remain key risks as the company executes its growth strategy.
